reason: Adding the nosuid mount option to /boot is a standard security hardening practice that prevents execution of setuid/setgid programs from the boot partition. This change is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage because /boot typically contains only kernel and bootloader files, not user-executable setuid binaries, and the remediation script includes a safety check to ensure /boot is defined in /etc/fstab before applying changes.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Low risk of service disruption; Dependent on /boot being correctly defined in /etc/fstab
